head: dense_fg_aux
hypothesis: E-46: Soft mask targets on true-mask samples (MoCA_Mask/CAD). Real PNG masks are Gaussian-blurred (sigma=1.0 at 28x28, ~3px transition zone) to soften boundaries; bbox-only MoCA CSV samples keep hard rectangles. Tests whether softening real mask boundaries helps the model learn gradual foreground transitions, improving large-object extent recovery (flatfish_2). Core prediction: softer boundaries provide better gradient signal for camouflaged object edges. If pf_mIoU > E-45 baseline OR per-video large-object mIoU improves, direction is valid.
